beats.activity: Sleep, rest after a steady day.
beats.reasoning: Seven hours forty-five minutes of sleep; Andre needs recovery from low energy and a full work day. Sleep ends at 06:30, leaving him refreshed for Friday morning.

diary:
Started the day with coffee on the patio—the weather was perfect, though I kept refreshing the transit alerts with that low-level anxiety everyone in the city seemed to share today. The strike meant I drove to Capitol Hill instead of taking the Link, which actually gave me time to think before the day really began.

The morning counseling sessions were full. One kid's parents are divorcing, another's struggling with friendship stuff. These conversations matter more than people realize. I grabbed a sandwich at Serious Pie around noon—ran into Tom from the district office, which was nice.

After school I ducked into Elliott Bay Books. Found a gorgeous collection of Mary Oliver poems I've been wanting. The staff there always knows what I'm looking for before I do. Stopped by the market on the way home, picked up some good tomatoes and basil.

Dinner on the patio was simple and quiet. Then I spent the evening in the studio with clay—my hands needed that. Something about shaping something with no agenda just felt right today.
thought_process: The transit strike actually freed me from my usual routine, which I think I needed. I could've stressed about the logistics, but instead I leaned into the unexpected—taking the drive gave me space to settle before work. The day was full of listening to kids' real problems, which reminded me why I do this, even when it's heavy. By afternoon I was ready to shift gears entirely. The bookstore felt like an oasis, and I deliberately chose leisure time over rushing home. Poetry and clay—both ways of letting my mind unfold without pressure. The mild weather helped too. I think I needed today to feel a little slower, a little more intentional, even with the city disrupted.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
